title: XML parsers should not be vulnerable to Denial of Service attacks
description: Improperly configured XML parsers can be exploited to perform Denial of Service (DoS) attacks, such as Billion Laughs or Quadratic Blowup attacks. Applications should ensure that XML parsers are configured to prevent excessive resource consumption and to reject maliciously crafted XML documents.
recommendation: Always configure XML parsers to limit entity expansion, disable DTDs if not needed, and set appropriate limits on input size and nesting. Use secure libraries and keep dependencies up to date.
impact: Exploiting this vulnerability can lead to application downtime, resource exhaustion, and potential service unavailability.

checks.message: Always configure XML parsers to limit entity expansion, disable DTDs if not needed, and set appropriate limits on input size and nesting. Use secure libraries and keep dependencies up to date.
